question_text: A student compares three ways to heat water: burning gas (82% efficient), electric heater (95% efficient), and solar panel (18% efficient). The student wants to heat water for 100 people daily using 10,000 J of energy. Which is the most economical choice, and why would someone still choose a less efficient option?
correct_answer: The electric heater is most efficient (95%), so it would produce 9,500 J of useful heat from 10,000 J input. However, people might choose gas heating due to lower initial cost, or solar panels to reduce electricity bills and environmental impact, even though they are less efficient.
explanation: This question requires students to evaluate efficiency calculations in a real-world context. The most efficient option is not always chosen because other factors like cost, availability, and environmental concerns matter to real consumers.
